score_scale: Absolute 1..7 per criterion; higher is better.

variants.notes: Clear source and scanable requirements, but the compiled output remains mostly a specification-shape prompt rather than a concrete integrated game design.
Rich, concrete browser-game specification with integrated crowd, factory, pushing, scope, validation, and acceptance details; source abstractions are useful though somewhat syntax-heavy.
Readable compact source and organized output, but much of the artifact restates required content instead of resolving mechanics into a specific design.
Reusable template source is abstract but coherent; output gives useful local-first, evidence, provenance, workspace, and validation obligations, though not a fully detailed app spec.

Very detailed implementation specification with domain entities, storage, workflows, UI, APIs, automation, privacy, and validation integrated into one artifact; dense source syntax adds cognitive load.
Plain source is easy to read, but the compiled output is only the compact request and provides almost no navigable implementation detail or reusable structure.
The source contains thoughtful tutoring constraints, but the output essentially remains the request rather than a pasteable adaptive tutor prompt.
Strong tutor prompt with stance, first interaction, adaptive branches, misconception diagnosis, practice ladder, feedback, remediation, and mastery loop tied to the source goals.
Friendly compact request is readable, but output is too thin to encode the requested adaptive pedagogy, mastery loop, or reusable teaching structure.
Clear compact brief, but output does not expand into gameplay, physics, progression, validation, or acceptance detail.
Comprehensive browser racing-game specification with concrete scope, controls, entities, course design, feedback, balance, assets, architecture, and real-browser validation.
Reusable template composition is evident and readable enough, but the output remains mostly template obligations rather than a concrete Orbital Drift design.
Compact source is clear, but compiled output is only the brief request and lacks gates, schemas, workflows, UI, automation, and validation substance.
Dense but coherent release command-center specification with gates, evidence, validation, decisions, actions, APIs, UI, automation, and audit validation integrated.
Template-based source and output provide useful release-readiness and workspace modules, but stay more generic than a full end-to-end implementation contract.

Strong research-brief instruction artifact with context, source families, evidence grading, alternatives, explainability, recency, and caveat rules integrated, though it is not an actual researched brief.
Readable compact request, but output is minimal and does not develop evidence standards, source mapping, comparisons, or decision-grade structure.
Reusable research template captures the topic, mission, context, and required sections cleanly, but remains a template rather than grounded findings or detailed synthesis.
Readable source and organized browser-game specification requirements, but the compiled artifact asks for a design more than it supplies concrete scenario rules.
Clear enough compact source and output, but integration stays at a requested-section level with limited concrete transit, growth, or swarm-rule resolution.

Most cohesive transit artifact: it ties routes, trails, congestion, growth, scoring, scenario layout, update order, actions, validation, and tuning into one required design, though many values are still to be filled in.
Readable compact concept, but output does not expand tower defense, deckbuilding, ecosystem simulation, UI, assets, validation, or acceptance criteria.
Reusable game templates are clearly assembled and scanable, but the output is mostly generic obligations rather than concrete Verdant Relay content and tuning.
Rich implementation spec integrating route defense, cards, ecosystem feedback, assets, UI, data structures, performance, validation, risks, and acceptance into a coherent first build.
Detailed local-first work-intelligence specification with monitoring, triage, cards, decisions, actions, outputs, alerts, APIs, persistence, UI, and validation coherently integrated.
Compact source is understandable, but output is only the brief request and lacks the structures needed for implementation or reuse.
Reusable workspace template gives a useful modular contract for monitoring, board states, outputs, APIs, events, and validation, but remains more generic than variant-specific implementation detail.
